title: Macleaya cordata

This family consists of about 111 genera and approximately 1,200 species. Macleaya cordata is further classified within the genus Macleaya, which is a group of flowering plants native to Asia. The genus Macleaya is often referred to as the plume poppy due to its showy, plume-like flowers. Macleaya cordata is also known as the white plume poppy due to its white, plume-like flowers.
